mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains above 100k signal robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) and low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 2%) signals central bank inaction against price pressures, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 0.5% in 70%+ of cases where both CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2%) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST > 1200k) during late-cycle expansion signals sustained demand momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns fall below 0.2% in 50%+ of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) indicates weakening supply chain capacity, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 0.3% in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines by >1% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly inflation (CPIAUCSL > 0.5% increase) signals potential central bank rate hikes, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL rises by >0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real sales (RSAFS > 5% increase) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS rises by >5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) and strong payroll gains (PAYEMS > 100k) signal a tight labor market that supports corporate pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns fall below 0.1% in 50%+ of instances where both UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 100k.
